properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: No help, variable specified on the command line.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: If this value is on, makefiles will be generated without the .SILENT directive, and all commands will be echoed to the console during the make.  This is useful for debugging only. With Visual Studio IDE projects all commands are done without /nologo.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Set USES_TERMINAL on specially marked tasks. This makes the task run in the foreground and can directly access stdio. This is helpful for debugging tasks that take a long time, or want to insert a breakpoint into the process. Note: Only one task can run in foreground at a time.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: (This variable does not exist and should not be used)

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: (This variable does not exist and should not be used)

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Insert aarch64 code to switch to aarch32. Requires the elfloader to be in EL2

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Work around for a Cortex-A9 errata. Derived from Linux kernel.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable the GPT physical timer access for NS PL1

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Perform a SHA256/MD5 Hash of the of each elf file that the elfloader checks on load

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Include DTB in the CPIO in case bootloader doesn't provide one

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Install SMC call handlers in monitor mode.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Place the rootserver images at the end of memory

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Builds the kernel with support for a userspace debug API, which can     allows userspace processes to set breakpoints, watchpoints and to     single-step through thread execution.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable hardware VFP and SIMD context switch         This enables the VFP and SIMD context switch on platforms with         hardware support, allowing the user to execute hardware VFP and SIMD         operations in a multithreading environment, instead of relying on         software emulation of FPU/VFP from the C library (e.g. mfloat-abi=soft).

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable SMMU interrupts.     SMMU interrupts currently only serve a debug purpose as     they are not forwarded to user level. Enabling this will     cause some fault types to print out a message in the kernel.     WARNING: Printing fault information is slow and rapid faults     can result in all time spent in the kernel printing fault     messages

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Architecture to use when building the kernel

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Disable the trapping of WFI     and WFE instructions when configuring the     Hyp Configuration Registor (HCR) of a VCPU

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable Cortex-A9 prefetcher     Cortex-A9 has an L1 and L2 prefetcher. By default     they are disabled. This config options allows     them to be turned on. Enabling the prefetchers     requires that the kernel be in secure mode. ARM     documents indicate that as of r4p1 version of     Cortex-A9 the bits used to enable the prefetchers     no longer exist, it is not clear if this is just     a document error or not.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable workaround for 430973 Cortex-A8 (r1p0..r1p2) erratum     Enables a workaround for the 430973 Cortex-A8 (r1p0..r1p2) erratum. Error occurs     if code containing ARM/Thumb interworking branch is replaced by different code     at the same virtual address.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable workaround for 773022 Cortex-A15 (r0p0..r0p4) erratum     Enables a workaround for the 773022 Cortex-A15 (r0p0..r0p4) erratum. Error occurs     on rare sequences of instructions and results in the loop buffer delivering     incorrect instructions. The work around is to disable the loop buffer

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: PL0 access to generic timer CNTPCT and CNTFRQ.     Grant user access to physical counter and counter     frequency registers of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens a timing     channel

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: PL0 access to PMU.     Grant user access to Performance Monitoring Unit.     WARNING: While useful for evaluating performance,     this option opens timing and covert channels.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: PL0 access to generic timer CNTP_CTL and CNTP_CVAL.     Grant user access to physical timer registers of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens a storage channel and allows threads to easily     corrupt these registers for each other, this should only be used for     debugging / development purposes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: PL0 access to generic timer CNTVCT and CNTFRQ.     Grant user access to virtual counter and counter     frequency registers of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens a timing     channel

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: PL0 access to generic timer CNTV_CTL and CNTV_CVAL.     Grant user access to virtual timer registers of the generic timer.     WARNING: selecting this option opens a storage channel and allows threads to easily     corrupt these registers for each other, this should only be used for     debugging / development purposes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable prefetch streams initated by STB access     Enable prefetch streams initated by STB access. Enabled is the reset value

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Prefetcher to initated on a ReadUnique or ReadShared     Sets prefetcher to initated on a ReadUnique (n) or ReadShared (y)     ReadUnique is the reset value

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Trap, but don't save/restore VCPUs' CP14 accesses     This allows us to turn off the save and restore of VCPU threads' CP14     context for performance (or other) reasons, we can just turn them off     and trap them instead, and have the VCPUs' accesses to CP14     intercepted and delivered to the VM Monitor as fault messages

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Build as Hypervisor. Utilise ARM virtualisation extensions to build the kernel as a hypervisor

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable SystemMMU for the Tegra TK1 SoC

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: When set the kernel will update the VOFFSET     register of a VCPU when restoring it so that its view of Virtual time hasn't increased while it     was suspended.  When unset the VOFFSET won't be updated other than by the read and write register api.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable benchamrks including logging and tracing info.     Setting this value > 1 enables a 1MB log buffer and functions for extracting data from it     at user level. NOTE this is only tested on the sabre and will not work on platforms with < 512mb memory.     This is not fully implemented for x86.     none -> No Benchmarking features enabled.     generic -> Enable global benchmarks config variable with no specific features.     track_kernel_entries -> Log kernel entries information including timing, number of invocations and arguments for     system calls, interrupts, user faults and VM faults.     tracepoints -> Enable manually inserted tracepoints that the kernel will track time consumed between.     track_utilisation -> Enable the kernel to track each thread's utilisation time.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Number of milliseconds until the boot thread is preempted.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: In debug mode, seL4 prints diagnostic messages to its serial output describing,     e.g., the cause of system call errors. This setting determines whether ANSI escape     codes are applied to colour code these error messages. You may wish to disable this     setting if your serial output is redirected to a file or pipe.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Size of DTB blob, in bytes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Adds a system call that allows users to specify code to be run in kernel mode.     Useful for profiling.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Replaces the undefined instruction handler with a call to a function pointer in r8.     This is an alternative mechanism to the code injection syscall. On ARMv6 the syscall     interferes with the caches and branch predictor in such a way that it is unsuitable     for benchmarking. This option has no effect on non-ARMv6 platforms.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable debug facilities (symbols and assertions) in the kernel

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Do not enable branch prediction (also called program flow control) on startup.     This makes execution time more deterministic at the expense of dramatically decreasing     performance. Primary use is for debugging.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Do not enable the L1 data cache on startup for debugging purposes.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Do not enable the L1 instruction cache on startup for debugging purposes.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Do not enable the L2 cache on startup for debugging purposes.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: On ia32 platforms, this option disables the L2 hardware prefetcher, the L2 adjacent     cache line prefetcher, the DCU prefetcher and the DCU IP prefetcher. On the cortex     a53 this disables the L1 Data prefetcher.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: A C file providing the symbols ksDomSchedule and ksDomeScheudleLength         to be linked with the kernel as a scheduling configuration.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: This option is a heuristic to attempt to detect when the FPU is no longer in use,    allowing the kernel to save the FPU state out so that the FPU does not have to be    enabled/disabled every thread swith. Every time we restore a thread and there is    active FPU state, we increment this setting and if it exceeds this threshold we    switch to the NULL state.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Enable -fwhole-program when linking kernel. This should work modulo gcc bugs, which     are not uncommon with -fwhole-program. Consider this feature experimental!

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: seL4 does not properly check for and handle spurious interrupts. This can result     in unnecessary output from the kernel during debug builds. If you are CERTAIN these     messages are benign then use this config to turn them off.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Allows the kernel to write the userError to the IPC buffer

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Use the MCS kernel configuration, which is not verified.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Max number of bootinfo untyped caps

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Maximum number of work units (delete/revoke iterations) until the kernel checks for    pending interrupts (and preempts the currently running syscall if interrupts are pending).

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: The number of priority levels per domain. Valid range 1-256

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Allow the kernel to print out messages to the serial console during bootup and execution.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Maximum size in bits of chunks of memory to zero before checking a preemption point.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Maximum number of objects that can be created in a single Retype() invocation.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Root CNode Size (2^n slots)     The acceptable range is 8-27 and 7-26, for 32-bit and 64-bit respectively.     The root CNode needs at least enough space to contain up to BI_CAP_DYN_START.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: This describes the log2 size of the kernel stack. Great care should be taken as    there is no guard below the stack so setting this too small will cause random    memory corruption

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: On a double fault the kernel can try and print out the users stack to aid     debugging. This option determines how many words of stack should be printed.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: When enabled this configuration option prevents the usage of any other options that    would compromise the verification story of the kernel. Enabling this option does NOT    imply you are using a verified kernel.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Multiplier to scale kernel WCET estimate by: the kernel WCET estimate       is used to ensure a thread has enough budget to get in and out of the       kernel. When running in a simulator the WCET estimate, which is tuned       for hardware, may not be sufficient. This option provides a hacky knob      that can be fiddled with when running inside a simulator.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: (This variable does not exist and should not be used)

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value:
This flag causes CMake to add lots of source files to its dependency lists
     which slows down its configuration times.
     When not making changes in the musllibc directory, it is likely not necessary
     to have this enabled.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Build libpicotcp with bsd socket support

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Function attributes     default->Verification friendly default configuration. syscalls will be inlined,         but generated functions will not.     inline->When set to true will mark generated functions as 'inline', allowing         them to be inlined by the callee user code. This may be undesirable         for verification, so setting to 'n' will forcibly prevent the function         from being inlined.     public->When set to true will make all user facing functions available as         public symbols, which can be convenient for some language bindings.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: use only IPC buffer for syscalls. When generating syscall wrappers, only use the     IPC buffer for marshalling and unmarshalling arguments. Without this option set,     arguments will be passed in registers where possible for better performance.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Default ZF_LOG_LEVEL for the ZF_LOG library. 5 is LOG_LEVEL_ERROR. 0 is     the most verbose setting.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Page size to be used for linker

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Size of the initial stack for the root task

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Size of static TLS area for new processes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Additional number of internal threads supported beyond those used for user processes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Capacity of fake page file in bytes

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Number of pages for each internal SOS stack

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Maximum length of filename plus one

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Maximum number of processes that can run at the same time

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: The name of the page file in NFS

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Use local FS on CPIO instead of NFS

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: (This variable does not exist and should not be used)

entries.name: config_configure_string
entries.value: #define CONFIG_ARM_HIKEY_OUTSTANDING_PREFETCHERS @KernelArmHikeyOutstandingPrefetchers@;#define CONFIG_ARM_HIKEY_PREFETCHER_STRIDE @KernelArmHikeyPrefetcherStride@;#define CONFIG_ARM_HIKEY_PREFETCHER_NPFSTRM @KernelArmHikeyPrefetcherNPFSTRM@;#define CONFIG_ARCH_AARCH64 1;#define CONFIG_SEL4_ARCH @KernelSel4Arch@;#define CONFIG_ARCH_ARM 1;#define CONFIG_ARCH @KernelArch@;#define CONFIG_ARM_SEL4_ARCH aarch64;#define CONFIG_WORD_SIZE 64;#define CONFIG_ARM_PLAT odroidc2;#define CONFIG_PLAT_ODROIDC2 1;#define CONFIG_PLAT @KernelPlatform@;#define CONFIG_ARM_CORTEX_A53 1;#define CONFIG_ARCH_ARM_V8A 1;#define CONFIG_ARM_MACH
